app.name: Teal
app.bestFor: Most complete all-in-one job hunt: resume builder plus a job tracker and keyword match scoring against listings.
app.rankBasis: The broadest app-track consensus: it appears in all three cited job-search roundups, serves 4M+ job seekers, and has 113 Trustpilot reviews alongside strong community recognition for combining resume tailoring with job tracking.
app.pricingFree: Free forever: unlimited resumes, downloads, and job tracking; limited surfaced match keywords plus starter AI-generation credits
app.pricingPaid: Teal+ $13/week - unlimited keywords, advanced analysis, and unlimited AI generation
pros: Reviewers consistently value having resume tailoring and the full job-search pipeline in one workspace
The free tier is unusually usable, with unlimited resumes, exports, and tracked jobs
cons: Independent reviews repeatedly flag the weekly subscription as expensive for a long job search
Billing and cancellation friction appears in a cluster of lower-rated community reviews
It organizes and improves applications but does not submit them

app.name: Rezi
app.bestFor: Deepest ATS-optimization focus, built around an internal ATS scoring system; a Reddit favorite for beating filters.
app.rankBasis: Its ATS specialization is corroborated by all three cited roundups and a larger independent footprint than most focused resume optimizers, including 254 Trustpilot reviews and repeated Reddit recommendations.
app.pricingFree: Free: 1 resume, limited AI tools, 1 standard template, and 3 PDF downloads
app.pricingPaid: Pro $29/month - unlimited resumes, AI use, templates, and downloads plus 1 human resume review each month
pros: Reviewers value the focused ATS workflow and clear keyword-targeting feedback
Automatic formatting makes it fast to produce a clean, conventional resume
The $149 lifetime option can be better value than recurring subscriptions
cons: Independent testing finds that AI text can be generic or inaccurate without substantial editing
The template library is plainer and less varied than design-led builders
The proprietary Rezi score should not be treated as a guarantee of ATS performance

app.name: Simplify
app.bestFor: Chrome extension that autofills applications across Greenhouse, Lever, Ashby, Workday and LinkedIn (you still hit submit).
app.rankBasis: The clear autofill leader in the set: it appears in all three cited automation roundups, while its Chrome extension reports a 4.9/5 aggregate rating from about 3,700 ratings and 500K+ users.
app.pricingFree: Free: unlimited Copilot autofill, job tracking, job matching, and core resume tools
app.pricingPaid: Simplify+ $19.99/week - AI resume tailoring, cover letters, application answers, networking outreach, and job analytics
features: AI application answers and tailored documents
pros: Users consistently praise the free extension for removing repetitive form entry
Particularly strong support for common tech ATS platforms such as Greenhouse, Lever, and Ashby
Applications remain human-reviewed because the user controls final submission
cons: It is autofill rather than true auto-apply, so users still find jobs and submit each form
Community testing reports less reliable autofill on complex enterprise and government forms
Paid-tier reviews mention high short-term pricing and AI drafts that still need editing

app.name: Kickresume
app.bestFor: Strongest AI first drafts for summaries and bullet points, with a large polished template library.
app.rankBasis: It is a recurring top-five pick in both cited builder roundups and has one of the category's strongest review signals, with a 4.6/5 Trustpilot score across roughly 3,800 reviews.
app.pricingFree: Free: 4 resume templates with free customization and downloads; verified students can access Premium at no charge
app.pricingPaid: Premium EUR19.20 for the first month - all AI tools, 40 premium templates, ATS checker, cover letters, and mobile access
pros: Large-scale review sentiment strongly favors its intuitive workflow and polished templates
AI drafting makes first versions fast, especially for summaries and experience bullets
Free Premium for verified students is a meaningful differentiator
cons: Many of the most useful templates and customization options require Premium
AI-generated phrasing can sound generic and needs factual, personal editing
Visual templates may require restraint when applying through conservative ATS workflows

app.name: Zety
app.bestFor: Cleanest template output and guided step-by-step building for a fast, polished-looking resume.
app.rankBasis: It remains a mainstream builder pick in both cited roundups and has the largest independent review volume here: a 4.0/5 Trustpilot score from more than 12,500 reviews and 10,000+ builder reviews cited on its site.
app.pricingFree: Free Basic package: build resumes and download plain-text files; PDF and Word exports require a paid plan
app.pricingPaid: Paid subscription required for PDF/Word downloads and premium features; current public pricing page does not expose a verifiable checkout price
pros: High-volume reviews consistently praise the beginner-friendly wizard and fast workflow
A large template and prewritten-content library helps users avoid a blank page
Real-time previews make design changes easy to understand
cons: Standard PDF and Word downloads are paywalled after the resume is built
Subscription and cancellation confusion is a recurring theme in negative Trustpilot reviews
Content suggestions can become generic unless users rewrite them around specific achievements

app.name: Sheets Resume
app.bestFor: Simple Google-Docs-style approach from an ex-recruiter; favored on Reddit for clean, no-nonsense resumes.
app.rankBasis: A niche community pick rather than a review-platform leader: its clean template is highlighted in the cited Reddit-derived roundup, but independent rating volume is limited compared with the five products above it.
app.pricingFree: Free: basic resume builder, resume template, and cover-letter generator
app.pricingPaid: Premium $39/month - unlimited use of the AI resume builder, cover letters, interview practice, and other job-search tools
pros: Community recommendations favor its simple, recruiter-led formatting over decorative templates
The core template and basic document tools can be used without paying
cons: Independent review volume is thin, so its broader product quality is less well established
The $39 monthly tier is expensive relative to more widely reviewed competitors
The design-first simplicity offers less template variety than larger builders

skill.name: Reactive-Resume
skill.bestFor: The gold-standard open-source resume builder — privacy-first, self-hostable, dozens of templates, one-click PDF export. The recognized category leader (~40k stars).
skill.rankBasis: The dominant open-source resume builder by adoption at 39,786 GitHub stars, more than 11,000 ahead of the next repository in this track, with frequent self-hosting recommendations in open-source communities.
pros: Category-leading community adoption and a polished visual editor
Privacy-friendly design with no ads, tracking, or required account
Broad export, language, import, and self-hosting support
cons: Self-hosting the full application requires infrastructure and ongoing maintenance
Community reports note that browser/PDF rendering can vary across templates and deployments
It builds documents but does not provide job matching or application workflow automation

skill.name: Awesome-CV
skill.bestFor: The iconic LaTeX résumé/CV template used across tech — clean, ATS-parseable typography (~28k stars).
skill.rankBasis: The most established LaTeX CV package in the track at 28,127 GitHub stars, with long-running Overleaf templates and widespread use across engineering and academic communities.
features: LaTeX resume, CV, and cover-letter templates
pros: Mature, widely recognized typography with reproducible PDF output
One source supports coordinated resumes, CVs, and cover letters
Version-controllable and highly customizable for LaTeX users
cons: Community discussions consistently cite the LaTeX learning curve and TeX installation burden
Its default multi-column visual style may need simplification for strict ATS parsing
Content editing lacks the live guided workflow of browser-based builders

skill.name: RenderCV
skill.bestFor: YAML-to-PDF résumé engine with version-controllable source and ATS-friendly output; hugely popular with engineers (~17k stars).
skill.rankBasis: The fastest-growing code-first resume engine in this set at 17,195 GitHub stars, with active releases, PyPI distribution, and repeated developer-community recommendations for YAML-based, version-controlled CVs.
features: Strict schema validation and editor autocomplete
pros: Users value separating structured resume content from presentation
Strong validation catches malformed data before PDF generation
Active development and version control suit engineering workflows
cons: YAML and command-line workflows are less accessible to nontechnical job seekers
Theme customization requires learning RenderCV configuration rather than direct visual editing
Users must run the tooling themselves and review generated PDFs after changes

skill.name: best-resume-ever (salomonelli)
skill.bestFor: Vue-based builder that generates multiple polished résumé designs from one data file (~16k stars).
skill.rankBasis: Its 16,460 GitHub stars show enduring interest, but it ranks below RenderCV because the repository still documents a Node 8.5-era Vue workflow and carries a much older maintenance profile.
pros: One data file can generate several distinct resume designs
The large star count and template contributions provide substantial community examples
Local generation keeps personal resume data under the user's control
cons: The documented Node 8.5/Vue toolchain is dated and can require dependency troubleshooting
Setup is developer-oriented compared with modern no-install builders
Several decorative and multi-column templates need ATS suitability checked individually

skill.name: OpenResume
skill.bestFor: Open-source résumé builder and parser focused on ATS compatibility; runs fully in your browser, no signup (~9k stars).
skill.rankBasis: A well-adopted ATS-first browser project at 8,771 GitHub stars, distinguished in community comparisons by combining a resume builder with a parser rather than offering templates alone.
pros: No signup and local-only processing are repeatedly highlighted as privacy strengths
The focused single-column design reduces formatting decisions and ATS risk
Import and parser tools help users diagnose existing resumes
cons: The deliberate single-template approach provides little visual variety
PDF import and parsing can require manual correction when source layouts are complex
It lacks integrated job tracking, cover letters, and application automation

skill.name: sb2nov/resume
skill.bestFor: Widely-forked LaTeX software-engineer résumé template; a de-facto GitHub starting point (~7k stars).
skill.rankBasis: A de-facto software-engineering template with 6,836 GitHub stars and extensive forks, Overleaf reuse, and Reddit references under the related 'Jake's Resume' style.
features: Documented education, experience, and project sections
pros: Community adoption shows the compact engineering format is proven and easy to recognize
The restrained one-column structure is generally straightforward for ATS parsers
Small source files are easy to version and customize for technical roles
cons: Editing requires LaTeX knowledge or an Overleaf workflow
The software-engineer structure needs significant adaptation for other professions
Manual spacing changes can make the template brittle as content length grows

skill.name: JSON Resume (resume-cli)
skill.bestFor: Official CLI for the JSON Resume standard — one JSON file, many community themes and export formats (~5k stars).
skill.rankBasis: The official CLI for the JSON Resume ecosystem has 4,720 GitHub stars and a durable standard behind it, but ranks seventh because active CLI development moved to the jsonresume.org monorepo in 2026.
pros: A portable open schema lets one resume feed many themes and tools
Validation and CLI automation fit version-controlled developer workflows
The revived Node 18+ package preserves a long-standing ecosystem
cons: The standalone repository is no longer the active development home, which can confuse contributors
Community themes vary widely in maintenance, rendering quality, and ATS behavior
JSON editing and command-line use are less approachable than a visual builder

skill.name: ResumeSkills (Paramchoudhary)
skill.bestFor: The most complete Agent Skills pack for job seekers: 20 SKILL.md skills covering ATS optimization, bullet writing, job-fit analysis, and interview prep (~1.3k stars).
skill.rankBasis: The strongest dedicated agent-skill pack in the track at 1,252 GitHub stars, with 20 documented career skills and independent coverage from Claude Code Playbooks, but far less adoption than the builder repositories above it.
pros: The 20-skill collection covers more of the job-search lifecycle than a single resume prompt
Modular SKILL.md files are easy to inspect, adapt, and invoke selectively
Workflows explicitly emphasize role-specific tailoring and ATS conventions
cons: Independent user-review volume remains limited beyond GitHub adoption and directory coverage
Output quality depends on the host model and still requires factual verification
Some workflows encourage estimating missing metrics, which users must constrain to avoid unsupported claims

skill.name: resume-cover-letter (jezweb/claude-skills)
skill.bestFor: Resume + cover-letter SKILL.md inside a popular general Claude Code skills/plugins collection (~940 stars overall).
skill.rankBasis: Its parent collection has 942 GitHub stars and 60 production-oriented skills, but this resume capability is one skill inside a broad plugin and has little resume-specific independent review evidence.
features: AU/NZ, US, and UK format guidance
pros: The instructions are unusually concrete about regional conventions and ATS-safe formatting
It requires target-role context and pushes output toward measurable achievements
The skill is transparent, editable, and easy to install with the broader writing plugin
cons: The star count measures the entire multi-purpose repository rather than this resume skill
There is little independent review volume for the resume workflow itself
It outputs Markdown guidance rather than a finished DOCX or PDF document

skill.name: proficiently-claude-skills (proficientlyjobs)
skill.bestFor: Claude Code skills for AI-powered job search, resume tailoring, and cover-letter writing with a structured per-application workflow (~300 stars).
skill.rankBasis: A promising but early dedicated workflow at exactly 300 GitHub stars, offering more application automation than the other skills but with the smallest adoption base and almost no independent review record.
features: Greenhouse, Lever, and Workday form filling
pros: The shared per-application workspace creates a coherent search-to-apply workflow
It combines document tailoring, contact matching, and form filling in one open plugin
Users retain inspectable local files for each application
cons: The 300-star project has too little independent usage evidence for high confidence
Setup spans personal files, browser automation, contacts, and optional Telegram integration
Automated searches, generated documents, and filled forms all require careful human review

notes: App track has strong, Reddit-corroborated consensus (Teal, Rezi, Simplify). Note that mass auto-apply bots like LazyApply are widely criticized (low Trustpilot scores, LinkedIn now flags 'human-impossible' velocity), so autofill-with-review tools (Simplify) are ranked over spray-and-pray automation. The Skill/Repo track is now ranked by real quality, reputation, and adoption (GitHub stars) rather than form factor — kind ('skill' vs 'repo') is only a tag. It mixes the notable open-source résumé repos (Reactive-Resume, Awesome-CV, RenderCV, best-resume-ever, OpenResume, sb2nov, JSON Resume) with the leading resume Agent Skills; Paramchoudhary's ResumeSkills is the strongest dedicated skill but now ranks below the far more adopted builder repos. Star counts verified via the GitHub API (Jul 2026); the tiny jananthan30/Resume-Builder (~64 stars) was dropped as below the quality bar.
